title: Lacto-Vegetarian Crunchy Canadians Vermicelli Pilaf
description: A spicy and crunchy monsoon-inspired Canadian dish featuring rice vermicelli, perfect for a quick and satisfying lacto-vegetarian meal. This dish combines the flavors of Canada with the comfort of a warm pilaf.

ingredients.name: Cauliflower florets
ingredients.notes: Use frozen or fresh, whichever is available.

ingredients.name: Brussels sprouts
ingredients.notes: Cut in half to ensure even cooking.

ingredients.name: Crispy fried shallots
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, whichever is convenient.

ingredients.name: Spicy maple syrup
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste for desired level of sweetness.

ingredients.name: Ground cumin
ingredients.notes: Add a smoky depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Chopped scallions
ingredients.notes: Green and white parts, for garnish.

ingredients.name: Diced bell peppers
ingredients.notes: Red, yellow, or orange, whichever is available.

instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the diced bell peppers and cook until slightly tender, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the diced carrots and cook until slightly tender, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the Brussels sprouts and cauliflower and cook until tender, about 6-7 minutes.
Add the cooked vermicelli and stir to combine.
Add the spicy maple syrup, smoked paprika, ground cumin, and salt. Stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a serving dish and garnish with chopped cilantro, lime juice, grated ginger, scallions, and sliced almonds.
